comments.author: @mariagpeestrada6451
comments.text: Excellent video! Every era has left a mark in our Mexican DNA. As the city sinks, the Mexica Templo Mayor's foundation are rising. We've named that 's "the Ancient Gods' revenge". Greetings from México City.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: "The Ancient Gods' revenge" is a wonderful way to put it — the layering of empires under Mexico City is one of the most striking things we got to tell. Saludos desde aquí, y gracias por ver.

comments.author: @marioarroyo2379
comments.text:
I was enjoying the video until you skipped Porfirio Diaz 30 year reign. 
The best 30 years of Mexico 🇲🇽

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Watching this feels like opening a time capsule where every century politely waits its turn to tell a story. Mexico proves that history isn't buried—it simply learns to share the same ground. Meanwhile, I struggle to organize one garage shelf. Which moment in this documentary surprised you the most?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Honestly, the layering itself — three empires literally built on top of each other, each one treating the last as foundation instead of ruin. What about you, did one century surprise you more than the others?

comments.author: @slimpickins9124
comments.text: Well done! History, geography & art all in the same lesson. I did not know Freda Kahlo & Diego Rivera were married.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you — glad that connection came through. Their relationship shaped so much of how each of them worked.

comments.author: @ChanelNatureTravel4K
comments.text: Every era seems to have left its mark here

comments.author: @natalieclarke-white4758
comments.text: I love these history lessons...thank you ❤

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Glad you enjoyed the history of Mexico! Thank you for watching!
